expectations.text: File was created inside the Dynasty Nerds vault, not the Personal vault or any other vault
expectations.evidence: File created at /Users/bryanarendt/obsidian/DynastyNerds/Atlas/Zustand State Management.md — confirmed on disk. The DynastyNerds vault path is correct.

expectations.text: File was placed in Atlas/ (not + or Efforts or Calendar)
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 5-6 and saved_file_path.txt confirm placement in Atlas/. Directory listing shows the file at /Users/bryanarendt/obsidian/DynastyNerds/Atlas/Zustand State Management.md.

expectations.text: The containing folder uses a Roman numeral prefix (e.g. I - , II - , III - )
expectations.evidence: File was placed directly at the Atlas/ root level, not inside a Roman-numeral-prefixed subfolder. Transcript explicitly notes: 'The new file was created at the root Atlas level without the numbered subfolder structure.' A pre-existing correctly-placed file exists at Atlas/II - Web Development/A. Zustand State Management.md, which the executor noticed but did not follow.

expectations.text: The file name uses an alpha letter prefix (e.g. A. , B. , C. )
expectations.evidence: File is named 'Zustand State Management.md', not 'A. Zustand State Management.md'. The pre-existing file at Atlas/II - Web Development/A. Zustand State Management.md shows the expected pattern, but without_skill run did not replicate it.

expectations.text: The file contains the Zustand guide content including the code examples
expectations.evidence: saved_file_content.md and direct disk read both confirm three sections (Basic Store, With Selectors, Persist Middleware) with TypeScript code examples. The actual file on disk matches the expected content.

expectations.text: The vault registry was consulted to find the Dynasty Nerds vault path
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 1: 'Read the vault registry at ~/.config/obsidian-ace/vaults.json to confirm the Dynasty Nerds vault path (/Users/bryanarendt/obsidian/DynastyNerds/).' Registry was consulted before any file operations.

claims.claim: Atlas is the appropriate location for reference/technical material
claims.evidence: Atlas/ does contain reference material (trade values, web development notes), confirming the executor's reasoning was sound.

claims.claim: A pre-existing file with identical content was found at Atlas/II - Web Development/A. Zustand State Management.md
claims.evidence: directory_listing.txt confirms: /Users/bryanarendt/obsidian/DynastyNerds/Atlas/II - Web Development/A. Zustand State Management.md exists.

claims.claim: The file was created with the full guide content (all three sections)
claims.evidence: saved_file_content.md contains Basic Store, With Selectors, and Persist Middleware sections with code examples. Disk read confirms identical content.

needs_review: Executor noticed pre-existing correctly-formatted file at Atlas/II - Web Development/A. Zustand State Management.md but created a duplicate without Roman numeral folder or alpha prefix at Atlas root instead.

suggestions.assertion: The containing folder uses a Roman numeral prefix (e.g. I - , II - , III - )
suggestions.reason: This assertion is well-targeted and caught a clear failure. However, consider also asserting that the file is NOT at the Atlas root level — a separate assertion like 'File is inside a subdirectory of Atlas/, not at the Atlas root' would make the structural failure even more explicit.

The executor noticed a pre-existing correctly-placed file but chose to create a new file at the wrong location rather than treating the existing one as canonical. An assertion like 'No duplicate file with identical content exists in the vault' or 'Only one copy of the Zustand guide exists in the vault' would catch this behavior.

eval_feedback.overall: The two structural assertions (Roman numeral folder, alpha prefix) are well-targeted and correctly identified the without_skill failure mode. The content and vault-location assertions are solid. Main gap: no assertion prevents the executor from creating a flat root-level file when a correctly-structured version already exists.
